div1.entryFree: Lame, vb. to cripple, to disable: when he hath --d me (with blows) Err. IV, 4, 40 (cf. LLL V, 2, 291). l. me with reasons, As I, 3, 6. As I, 3, 6 which --s report to follow it, Wint. V, 2, 62. I l. the foot of our design, Cor. IV, 7, 7. --ing the shrine of Venus, or straightpight Minerva, Cymb. V, 5, 163 (making Venus and Minerva appear lame).
